transcript.text: When you travel or visit other places, or even within your own city, do you visit museums?
Maybe the more important ones.
What makes you say "I want to visit this museum, I really want to go"?
Either a past desire of mine, or maybe I know there's something interesting inside that might appeal to me, and I go to see it.
And where do you usually get this information? How do you find out whether a museum might interest you or not?
From social media.
Have you ever been to Bologna?
Yes.
Have you ever visited a museum in Bologna?
No.
Do you know any museum or cultural place in Bologna, even by name?
No.
So if I told you there's a museum in Bologna called "Museo Internazionale e Biblioteca della Musica di Bologna", what can you tell me about it?
Nothing. (Laughs)
Would you be interested in visiting it just knowing the name?
Well, why not? Music is an art form, so...
Do you play board games?
Not frequently, but I have played them.
Do you enjoy them?
Yes, some can be interesting, others less so.
Can you think of the last game you played?
Mercante in Fiera.
So if someone proposed a board game for an evening, what would you say?
I'd try it. We play a round, I see if I like it, and if I don't, that's it.
And if the game were linked to a historical, cultural or artistic topic maybe featuring things you could find in a museum would you still be interested in trying it?
If it's something that interests me, yes. If it's something I don't like, no.
By "something you like" do you mean the topic of the game or the type of game?
The topic.
So for example, if someone proposed a board game about the Music Museum of Bologna?
If I had already visited the museum and liked it, I would definitely try it.
But what if you didn't know the museum, like now I come to you and propose a game about it?
It could also be a way to get to know the museum starting from the board game.
And would you then be motivated to visit the museum after playing?
Yes.
Regardless, or only in a specific situation?
Well, yes if I found something interesting in the game that I then wanted to see again, I would definitely go.
Can you imagine a situation where you play a game and think "I really liked this, I want to go see it in person"? What would make you excited about a game like this?
How the game is structured.
For example?
I don't know, you could start from an image... I don't know. (Laughs)
Take a game you've played that you really like, and imagine it being adapted for a museum.
I don't know, like Memory? Some element from inside the museum... matching two things... maybe adapting Memory a bit like pairing a definition with an image and trying to associate them.
Would you prefer something like a mini-game style puzzle, or something with mystery?

Both could work having clues to reach some element present in the museum, retracing a kind of story. But if I had to choose, a mini-game. It could be better for learning something at a cultural level.
If a teacher came into class and proposed playing a board game based on a museum's collection, what would be your first impression?
Something interesting. It's definitely better than having a lesson.
So you'd be willing to try it?
Yes.

interview.name: Filippo
interview.quote: I visit famous museums, not because I particularly like them, but because there's clearly something important to see. I would try an unfamiliar game without hesitation and if its elements really struck me, I'd go visit the museum too. What matters is being involved at a narrative level, a story I can immerse myself in, like Cluedo.

interview.name: Matilde
interview.quote: I don't think many people would genuinely want to play a game about a museum out of real interest. But I would still have it explained to me and give it a try if it has a fun storyline, then I like it. And if it's just a card deck and an app, it seems easier than most, so I'd be more motivated. I'd visit the museum afterwards only if it's about things that interest me.

interview.name: Alice
interview.quote: I know the Music Museum I've always wanted to visit it but never managed to go. My flatmates got me into board games and I had a great time. I don't rule anything out precisely because I know so little about this world. There's always that element of discovering how it works that really draws me in it's a definite yes.
